raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "self-affirmation and behavioral commitment to epistemic autonomy and internal validation",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "an internal compass exists and is trustworthy for discerning truth",
    "external agreement is not required to maintain alignment with truth",
    "clarity can be cultivated as a stable internal state independent of others",
    "prior dependence on others' agreement was a barrier to personal truth"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent; the claims form a consistent progression from self-reliance to non-attachment to external consensus and cultivation of private clarity",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the speaker benefits from reduced external validation-seeking, possesses reasonable self-awareness to calibrate the internal compass, and operates in contexts where independent judgment is advantageous",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the internal compass encodes unexamined biases or distortions, valuable external feedback is systematically dismissed, or the commitment is used to rationalize isolation from corrective social input"
}
